title: Bajrang Dal men barge into Dehradun showroom, write ‘jihadi’ on managers’ name tags
content: Bajrang Dal members barged into a Dehradun footwear showroom, alleging Muslim managers harassed Hindu staff and deducted salaries. A video circulating on social media purportedly shows the group confronting Muslim employees and defacing their name tags. Police have not yet registered an FIR, awaiting a formal complaint.

title: Watch: How railway staff quickly rescued passengers from burning Rajdhani Express
content: A fire erupted in a Rajdhani Express coach in Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh early Sunday morning. All 68 passengers were safely evacuated from the B-1 coach within 15 minutes. The blaze also damaged the luggage van. The affected coach was detached. Five railway staff were injured in a separate accident during rescue operations. Train traffic on the Mumbai-Delhi route was affected.

title: PM Modi’s Netherlands visit: The dam India may look to for water lessons
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to the Afsluitdijk dam in the Netherlands highlighted Dutch expertise in water management, relevant to India's ambitious Kalpasar Project. This mega-project aims to create a freshwater reservoir in Gujarat, addressing water scarcity and flood protection, mirroring the multi-functional Dutch approach to climate resilience.

title: Watch: Bangladesh's viral buffaloes resembling Trump, Netanyahu steal spotlight
content: Albino buffaloes resembling Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu are drawing large crowds in Bangladesh ahead of Eid al-Adha. One buffalo, named Trump, is noted for its calm demeanor and golden hair, while another, named Netanyahu, is described as aggressive and mischievous. These unusual animals have become a significant attraction at cattle farms near Dhaka.

title: From July 10, US can deny your visa for this one reason
content: USCIS now has the power to reject or deny immigration applications, even keeping fees, if an invalid signature is found months or years later. This new rule, effective July 10, 2026, applies broadly to most benefit requests, including visas and Green Cards. Applicants cannot usually fix signature errors after submission.

title: 'Leader of opposition has right to suggest': BJP ally on Rahul Gandhi's NEET charges
content: The BJP has responded to Rahul Gandhi's demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. Party leaders stated that while the opposition has the right to suggest, the government is committed to dismantling the leak network and ensuring accountability. Investigations are ongoing, with arrests made.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: TVK floor test support sparks row: Dhinakaran warns expelled MLA S Kamaraj of disqualification
content: TTV Dhinakaran announced AMMK MLA S Kamaraj faces disqualification for supporting the ruling TVK government during a floor test. Dhinakaran accused Kamaraj of betrayal and stated the party will pursue disqualification, calling the TVK's acceptance of support 'horse-trading'. He removed Kamaraj from the party, vowing to take necessary steps.

title: Bill Gates' Foundation sold every last Microsoft share it owned
content: The Bill Gates Foundation Trust sold its entire Microsoft (MSFT) stake in Q1 2026, offloading the final 7.7 million shares worth $3.2 billion. The exit caps a two-year wind-down from 28.5 million shares, funding Gates' plan to spend the endowment by 2045. Microsoft stock slipped to $422.07 even as Pershing Square's Bill Ackman disclosed a fresh $2.3 billion MSFT buy the same day.

title: 'Eliminated totalling error': Education Ministry defends 'OSM' amid Class 12 result row
content: Following CBSE Class 12 results, Secretary Sanjay Kumar addressed concerns about the pass percentage drop, attributing it to the On-Screen Marking (OSM) system. While OSM, first introduced in 2014, was successfully reimplemented this year, eliminating totalling errors, around 13,000 answer sheets faced legibility issues due to light ink, requiring manual re-evaluation.

title: 'Sarpanch' Shreyas reaches another milestone, joins Dhoni & Kohli to become ...
content: The milestone came during Punjab Kings’ IPL 2026 match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Stadium in Dharamsala.

title: 'Why haven't you sacked education minister?' Rahul slams PM Modi over NEET-UG paper leak
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized Prime Minister Modi over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, questioning the inaction of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Gandhi drew parallels to the 2024 leak, highlighting repeated paper leaks and the government's response. The NEET-UG 2026 exam was cancelled due to paper leaks, affecting 23 lakh aspirants, with the CBI investigating the matter.

title: Why is RCB captain Rajat Patidar not playing vs Punjab Kings? - Explained
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru are without captain Rajat Patidar for their IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings, with Jitesh Sharma leading the side. Patidar is recovering well and is expected to return for the next match in Hyderabad. RCB currently sits atop the IPL 2026 points table.

title: Hyderabad man who slit woman’s throat dies after mob assault
content: A 28-year-old man, Teja, accused of murdering 22-year-old Vaishnavi in Jadcherla by slitting her throat, has died. He was assaulted by local residents after the attack and succumbed to his injuries while undergoing treatment. Police are investigating both the murder and the subsequent mob violence.

title: After Rajdhani Express blaze, relief vehicle rushing to spot flips into gorge in MP
content: A fire on the Rajdhani Express in Ratlam district was averted with all 68 passengers evacuated safely. However, a relief vehicle en route to the scene met with an accident, injuring over five railway employees. The incident disrupted traffic on the Delhi-Mumbai route for hours.

title: 2 Jodhpur sisters die by suicide within 2 months after years of gangrape, blackmail
content: Two sisters have died by suicide in Jodhpur's Kherapa area. The younger sister died Friday after consuming pesticide atop a water tank. She had accused eight people of gangrape and blackmail. Her elder sister died by suicide in March. The family alleges police inaction despite an FIR being lodged. Protests have erupted demanding action against accused and negligent police officials.

title: Ebola virus outbreak: WHO declares international health emergency after rare strain kills 87 in Congo
content: The World Health Organization declared an international health emergency over a rare Bundibugyo Ebola strain in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with dozens dead. Cases and deaths are rising, particularly in Congo's Ituri province, where community spread and violence complicate containment efforts. This marks Congo's 17th Ebola outbreak.

title: ‘Straight away’: Ravi Shastri backs Sooryavanshi for India debut next month
content: Former India coach Ravi Shastri believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for a senior India debut, potentially as early as next month on the Ireland tour. Shastri urged selectors to fast-track the Rajasthan Royals opener into the T20 setup, citing his impressive IPL form and fearless batting against experienced bowlers.

title: ‘Recipe for ecological disaster’: Congress writes to Rajnath Singh on Great Nicobar project
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh has urged Defence Minister Rajnath Singh to reconsider the Great Nicobar Island Project, deeming it an "ecological disaster." He argued that while strengthening defence is crucial, the project's commercial aspects outweigh security needs and could harm the region's ecology and tribal rights. Ramesh proposed alternative infrastructure expansions with lower environmental impacts.

title: ‘Tum karlo jo karna hai’: Man caught urinating inside Delhi Metro lift
content: An elderly man urinated in a Delhi Metro lift at Kalkaji station, sparking outrage. Police stated he experienced a medical emergency and couldn't find a toilet, leading to a penalty. The incident highlights concerns about public hygiene and the need for accessible facilities.

title: Nepal’s ‘Everest Man’ breaks his own record, scales Mount Everest for record 32nd time
content: Kami Rita Sherpa has set a new world record by summiting Mount Everest for the 32nd time, while Lhakpa Sherpa achieved her 11th ascent, extending her record for most climbs by a woman. These achievements highlight Nepal's rich mountaineering history amidst a busy climbing season.

title: 'Silence of virgin can be treated as consent': Taliban legalises child marriages with special rules
content: The Taliban regime in Afghanistan has enacted a new family law regulation, "Principles of Separation Between Spouses," which has drawn international criticism. A particularly controversial article interprets a virgin girl's silence after puberty as consent to marriage. The decree also addresses child marriage, divorce, and other marital disputes, granting fathers and grandfathers significant authority.

title: John Lennon’s final words revealed in new Cannes documentary by Steven Soderbergh
content: Steven Soderbergh's documentary beautifully encapsulates John Lennon’s final moments in a revealing interview conducted mere hours before his tragic passing. The intimate dialogue with Yoko Ono navigates complex topics such as human experience, societal change, and Lennon’s deep reflections on the concept of death.

title: ‘I had to see him’: Ganguly reveals why he briefly delayed Dhoni’s India call-up
content: Sourav Ganguly revealed he personally scouted MS Dhoni, travelling to Jamshedpur to witness his domestic prowess before his national selection.  Ganguly emphasised fast-tracking exceptional talent, believing it crucial for player development.  This philosophy, coupled with Saba Karim's initial praise for Dhoni's six-hitting ability, led to his swift integration into the Indian team, shaping a legendary career.

title: Demolition drive turns violent in Lucknow; lawyers pelt stones, police resort to lathicharge
content: Tensions flared in Kaiserbagh as police lathi-charged protesting advocates during a Municipal Corporation demolition drive. The operation targeted chambers near the Civil Court, ordered by the Lucknow Bench of the High Court to remove illegal encroachments. Police stated the situation is under control.

title: Chicago Bears and Dallas Cowboys could pursue the $123 million Steelers linebacker
content: The Pittsburgh Steelers could face a difficult decision after the 2026 NFL season as trade rumors around T.J. Watt continue to grow. With Nick Herbig due for a new contract and Alex Highsmith already on a big deal, several teams reportedly believe Pittsburgh may eventually consider moving Watt. The Chicago Bears and Dallas Cowboys stand out as possible landing spots because both teams still need another elite pass rusher. Watt’s contract, age and Pittsburgh’s long-term cap plans have now turned one of the league’s top defenders into a name to watch closely in upcoming NFL trade rumors.

title: Man slits woman’s throat in full public view for rejecting his proposal in Telangana
content: A 21-year-old receptionist, Y Vaishnavi, was tragically murdered in Jadcherla after rejecting a man's persistent harassment. The accused allegedly slit her throat in public view shortly after she disembarked from a bus. Despite immediate medical attention, she succumbed to her critical injuries.

title: CNG prices in Delhi-NCR rise again: Second hike in 48 hours — Check rates in your city
content: Delhi commuters face a double blow as CNG prices surged by Re 1, pushing the cost to Rs 80.09 per kg, the second hike in 48 hours. This follows a Rs 2 increase on Friday. The price hikes impact private vehicle owners, cabbies, and public transport users.

title: 'We welcome you': North Korean women football team arrives in South for a rare match
content: North Korea's Naegohyang Women's FC arrived in South Korea for the Asian Champions League semi-finals, marking the first sports delegation visit in eight years. The team will face South Korea's Suwon FC Women, with high public interest despite ongoing political tensions between the two nations.

title: Babar Azam breaks Ben Stokes' WTC record
content: Babar Azam's resilient 68 guided Pakistan through a tough spot on Day 2 against Bangladesh, marking his return from injury. This crucial half-century propelled him past Ben Stokes in the ICC World Test Championship's most fifty-plus scores list, showcasing his consistent performance in the prestigious tournament.

title: FPIs pull Rs 27,000 crore in May; 2026 outflows cross Rs 2.2 lakh crore mark
content: Foreign investors have continued their withdrawal from Indian equities, with net outflows reaching Rs 27,048 crore this month. This selling spree, totaling Rs 2.2 lakh crore in 2026, is driven by global macroeconomic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, and a stronger US dollar making developed markets more attractive.

title: Market recap: Top 10 firms bleed Rs 3.12 lakh crore in market cap; Reliance biggest loser
content: Indian markets experienced a significant downturn last week, with the top 10 most valued firms losing Rs 3.12 lakh crore. Geopolitical tensions, a weakening rupee, and rising inflation, exacerbated by surging crude oil prices, drove the decline. Reliance Industries saw the steepest fall, while Bharti Airtel was the sole gainer among the giants.

title: Shreyas Iyer on the verge of history, set to become 5th captain to...
content: Shreyas Iyer is set to captain Punjab Kings in his 100th IPL match, a significant milestone. He joins an elite group of captains with this achievement.  Iyer boasts an impressive win percentage and has led three different franchises to IPL finals.  This landmark game comes as PBKS battles to revive their playoff hopes after a string of defeats.

title: 'Unparalleled momentum to India-Netherlands friendship': PM Modi on talks with Dutch PM Rob Jetten
content: Prime Minister Modi's Netherlands visit concluded with a strategic partnership upgrade, marked by 17 key outcomes. A significant development includes Tata Electronics' pact with ASML for a semiconductor facility in Dholera, boosting India's ambitions. Modi also lauded the Indian diaspora's role in strengthening bilateral ties.

title: Dalal Street this week: Middle East cues, rupee and more — what will guide stock market?
content: Indian markets brace for a volatile week, with the US-Iran conflict and soaring crude oil prices dominating investor sentiment. A weakening rupee, coupled with inflation concerns, adds to the pressure. Analysts anticipate heightened sensitivity to geopolitical developments, especially concerning the Strait of Hormuz, and will closely watch foreign investor activity and upcoming economic data.

title: West Bengal committed to handover land to BSF within 45 days for border fencing: Minister
content: West Bengal minister Ashok Kirtania said land acquisition in most parts of the North 24 Parganas district was progressing satisfactorily, but flagged resistance in the Basirhat area

title: Sasthra Vedhi calls for green election norms, cites ‘Puthuppally model’ as blueprint for low-carbon poll campaigns
content: Call to establish a maximum ceiling on carbon footprint of candidates contesting in elections, mirroring the existing limits on financial expenditure

title: Kerala State Electricity Board eyes power procurement to meet peak demand during mid-June to December
content: Power utility is seeking 400 MW for second half of June as well as for August and September, 300 MW in July, and 500 MW each in October, November and December

title: Vessel carrying 20,000 tonnes of LPG reaches Gujarat after crossing Strait of Hormuz
content: The Marshall Islands-flagged MV SYMI started its journey from Qatar and docked at the port in Kandla around 11.30 p.m. on May 16 after crossing the Strait of Hormuz on May 13

title: India’s gems, jewellery exports down 9.07% in April at $2,226.45 million
content: Gold jewellery exports was down 21.77% to $841.54 million in April, compared to $1,075.67 million for the same period last year

title: Internal deformation detected within ‘rigid’ Indian plate: New study
content: High-precision GPS observations reveal distinct crustal movement patterns within the Indian tectonic plate. Researchers say long-term stresses from the India–Eurasia collision may influence earthquake activity inside the Indian plate

title: Why isn't PM sacking Education Minister Pradhan despite repeated paper leaks, asks Rahul
content: Mr. Gandhi also alleged that the "BJP-RSS nexus" has destroyed India's education system and said the hard work of 22 lakh NEET aspirants has gone to waste

title: Greater Nicobar project a recipe for ecological disaster: Jairam Ramesh
content: The Central government has been citing strategic interests for going ahead, for which the Congress leader has suggested alternative means in a letter the Defence Minister

title: A.P. CM Naidu’s call for third child is ‘pragmatic’, says TDP MP
content: Fertility rates have fallen below replacement levels in southern States and is a matter of serious concern for the country’s long-term economic growth, Tenneti Krishna Prasad said

title: Heavy security, elaborate traffic curbs in place for Kerala Cabinet swearing-in
content: Organisers are anticipating an influx of around 1 lakh people to witness one of the largest political gatherings the State has seen in recent years

title: NCB cracks major India-Myanmar drug syndicate, arrests kingpin
content: Officials say Chintuang emerged as one of the biggest traffickers operating along the Myanmar-Mizoram-Manipur-Assam-Tripura corridor and was identified as a major international supplier of methamphetamine and heroin

title: Kishan Reddy urges clean-up of electoral rolls, warns against obstruction
content: The Union Minister said no attempts to obstruct the EC’s work would be tolerated and also cautioned against any effort to intimidate government officials

title: PM Modi tours iconic Dutch dam; eyes cooperation in water management
content: Calling the Afsluitdijk Dam a “symbol of excellence and innovation”, the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) highlighted its role in water management, flood protection and freshwater storage

title: Why has the RSS called for dialogue with Pakistan?
content: What did RSS leader Dattatreya Hosabale say about ties with Pakistan and what reactions did his remarks trigger in India and Pakistan? Can his statement be read as reflecting government policy? What has the RSS’s stance on talks with Pakistan been?

title: PM Modi Sweden visit LIVE: PM Modi to head to Sweden on third-leg of 5-nation tour after Netherlands
content: PM Modi will hold talks with Swedish counterpart Ulf Kristersson and interact with members of the European Round Table for Industry in Sweden

title: Kerala Cabinet Formation LIVE: State police chief meets Ramesh Chennithala amid buzz over Home portfolio
content: Full list of Ministers to be finalised today; 21-member Cabinet with CM-designate Satheesan will take oath of office on May 18

title: Telangana health department warns of heatstroke symptoms as temperatures set to rise further
content: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a gradual increase of 2℃ to 3℃ in maximum temperatures across Telangana over the coming days

title: One year after Gulzar Houz fire, old risks continue to haunt Hyderabad’s historic core
content: Unsafe buildings, poor fire preparedness and congested lanes continue to pose risks across Hyderabad’s old city

title: Excess weight, stress leading to hypertension among all age groups: IMA former president Dr. V.S.Prasad
content: Speaking to media in Vizianagaram, Dr. Prasad said that people of all groups had become victims of hypertension due to changes in working hours, lack of sleep and eating junk fund regularly

title: Over 1,000 Telugu expats participate in Mahanadu by NRI TDP Saudi Arabia chapter in Dammam
content: Special guests Bode Prasad and TDP leader Mahasena Rajesh interacted with the Telugu diaspora and praised efforts to preserve Telugu culture overseas

title: CBSE Class 12 results: Education Ministry dismisses concerns about ‘On-Screen Marking’ affecting students’ scores
content: Education Ministry says ‘On Screen Marking’ (OSM) evaluation system is an internationally accepted norm; CBSE reduces revaluation and answer sheet verification charges to ₹100

title: Nellore TDP leaders expect to host Mahanadu next year
content: Local leaders and cadre of TDP were upset with the decision of Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu Naidu on holding the annual conclave virtually instead of conducting a grand event in Nellore

title: Summer festival season chokes Doddabetta Peak, conservationists call for curbs
content: The Nilgiris district police tried to implement curbs to ease traffic flow to the peak, by forcing visitors to take shuttle buses to the peak from the Doddabetta Junction

title: HRF alleges unchecked industrial effluent discharge in Anakapalli district
content: Residents alleged that repeated complaints over foul-smelling effluents, groundwater contamination, crop damage, destruction of aquatic life and adverse health impacts had failed to evoke any meaningful response from the authorities

title: I did not try to broker a deal between DMK and AIADMK: Rajinikanth
content: Rajinikanth asked how he could harbour envy against Mr. Vijay when there is a big generation gap between them

title: Phase 3 of SIR in 16 States, three UTs from May 30, says ECI
content: The Special Intensive Revision is being conducted to ensure that only eligible voters are included in the electoral rolls, says Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar

title: Petroleum dealers blame institutional procurement for fuel crisis
content: The outlets belonging to the Government Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) witnessed unusually high HSD sale volumes compared to their historical averages recently

title: Capt. Chowta urges NHAI to immediately construct missing service road link at Bannadka on NH 169
content: Because of the missing link, several mishaps, fatal as well as non-fatal, have been occurring the four-lane Bikarnakatte-Sanur NH, the MP says

title: SriLankan Airlines accuses Indian employees of ₹22 million fraud
content: It said that Indian law enforcement has been contacted, and investigations are currently ongoing to recover the loss.

title: Plight of betel leaf farmers miserable as gale storms hit fields, expect new government to give relief swiftly
content: The plight of the farmers’ was miserable that most of them are facing ‘red’ and were unable to stop the rising losses due to multiple factors

title: Kerala Chief Minister-designate Satheesan rejects attempts to ‘demonise’ IUML as anti-secular
content: Mr. Satheesan said government formation talks had “passed an important stage” and there was no roadblock to naming the cabinet

title: V.D. Satheesan | The ‘Nehruvian’ revivalist
content: A self-described supporter of Nehruvian socialism, the Congress leader who steered the party-led alliance, out of power for a decade, to an emphatic victory over the Left Democratic Front in Kerala, is now set to take the reins of the State

title: Disappointment writ large on the face of TDP’s Nellore cadre as Mahanadu goes hybrid
content: This ‘austerity measure’ was termed as in tune with the call given by the Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu

title: Major anti-encroachment drive conducted to clear out Howrah station area in Bengal
content: Many temporary stalls and structures occupying footpaths and public spaces near the bus stand and Ganga ghat area were removed using bulldozers and earthmovers as part of a railway land clearance initiative for passengers' convenience

title: Cabinet approves ₹7.42 crore ancillary works at Kukke Subrahmanya to be undertaken by Temple management
content: The works include construction of four public toilet blocks in the temple town and a well-equipped Go Shala

title: Kamal meets Vijay, submits 6 demands Tamil film industry’s development
content: The meeting comes a day after the MP called for a conversation with stakeholders regarding the adoption of ‘practical and sustainable film-making practices’

title: Kerala Cabinet: Congress leadership in talks with UDF allies to seal allocation of ministerial berths
content: Chief Minister-designate V.D. Satheesan’s office confirmed that the Congress has allocated five Cabinet berths to the IUML, the second-largest constituent of the UDF

title: HeLa to a new world where progress in science matches dignity for patient
content: The omission of Henrietta Lacks, who donated the immortal cells that have facilitated biomedical research, from scientific history is a rare wrong that has been righted, recently. Thanks to the battle her family fought, today, more regulations will govern ethical research.

title: Bengal govt. gives nod for handover of seven national highway stretches to NHAI, NHIDCL
content: These stretches were under the national highway wing of the State PWD, and the handover proposals had been pending for nearly a year despite repeated requests, the statement said

title: Suspend collection of payment over agricultural loans; waive off loans: Vasan urges T.N. Government
content: The TMC president’s statement follows representation from farmer associations calling for suspension of collection of payments over agricultural loans until they are waived

title: Congress, Manipur group question appointment of National Sports University V-C
content: A retired IPS officer has been installed in violation of recruitment rules and prescribed qualifications under an Act governing India’s only such university, they say

title: CNG prices hiked second time in 48 hours in Delhi amid increased input cost pressures
content: CNG is now priced from at ₹80.09 per kilogram in the NCT of Delhi with the latest hike. On May 15, 2026 the  prices were hiked by ₹2

title: President Murmu promulgates ordinance to increase Supreme Court judge strength by four
content: The current ordinance has amended Section 2 of the Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Act, 1956 to replace the word ‘thirty-three’ with ‘thirty-seven’

title: Anagha from Tirthahalli emerges as the State topper in SSLC after revaluation
content: Anagha, daughter of Shivakumar and Mangala, both teachers, had scored 100% in every subject except Social Science, where she received 99

title: Granite quarrying ‘kicking up dust’
content: The villages of Kuppam, Bangarupalem and Palamaner in Andhra Pradesh’s Chittoor district have been witnessing year-round granite mining activities. Although a regulatory system is in place, many operators resort to illegal quarrying which has repeatedly come under the scrutiny of the National Green Tribunal. The issue even drew political attention when TDP chief N. Chandrababu Naidu inspected quarries a few years ago, and expressed concern over unchecked mining, environmental damage and revenue loss, reports K. Umashanker

title: BRICS is evolving with new members; differences will take time to resolve: Brazil FM Mauro Vieira
content: UAE-Iran tensions over the war hold up consensus at BRICS FM meet; could spell trouble for BRICS Summit in September

title: ₹25-crore KERA project to modernise Wayanad coffee sector
content: The project also aims to ensure the quality of coffee saplings by providing financial assistance of ₹6 lakh each to 30 nurseries producing high-quality certified saplings

title: TCS sexual harassment case: Court says clear evidence of abetment by POSH committee member
content: The court had refused bail to TCS site head and POSH committee member Ashwini Chainani, others in the alleged sexual harassment-conversion case

title: Congress appears locked in last-minute consultations on government formation
content: The politics of factions appeared to play spoilsport, with AICC general secretary in charge of Kerala, Deepadas Munsi, reportedly making haste to prod the faction chiefs into a lasting consensus on cabinet formation before the deadline for submitting the final list of ministers to Kerala Governor Rajendra Arlekar expires at 2 p.m.

title: Man Hangs Self After Getting Slapped By Woman In Public In Maharashtra: Cops
content: A man allegedly died by suicide in Maharashtra's Beed district after he was reportedly slapped by a woman in the presence of others, which left him deeply distressed, a police official said on Sunday.

title: In Bizarre Case, Karnataka Shopkeeper Alleges Gang Hypnotised Him, Looted Cash
content: The police said the accused then handed over two flowers to a shopkeeper, allegedly claiming it would bring prosperity and well-being to him and his children

title: Lashkar Terrorist's Mission Detour: A Hair Transplant In Kashmir
content: Infiltrating Jammu and Kashmir to execute terror attacks for the banned Lashkar-e-Taiba (LET), a Pakistani terrorist paused his operations to fulfil a personal dream -- getting a hair transplant.

title: No Doctor, Locked Gate: Faridabad Woman Gives Birth In Hospital Parking Lot
content: The woman delivered a baby boy. Both the mother and newborn were later shifted inside the hospital and are reported to be stable.

title: NP Narvekar, Behind Harvard's $57 Billion Endowment Fund, Plans To Retire
content: Narvekar also serves as a trustee at The Chapin School and is part of the investment committee at Haverford College.

title: In 'Cut Money' Outburst, Exploited Villagers Target Trinamool Leaders
content: Several instances have surfaced across Bengal of the exploited people targeting the houses of Trinamool leaders and demanding the return of 'cut money'.

title: Delhi To Face Severe Heatwave Soon, Temperature May Hit 45 Degrees Celsius
content: Heatwave conditions are expected to prevail till May 22 across Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Chhattisgarh, and parts of Konkan and Goa.

title: Asked To Give Way, 2 Men Stop Bengaluru Woman's Range Rover, Assault Her Husband
content: Rachana alleged in her complaint that the accused were under the influence of alcohol and used vulgar language.

title: 'Jilted Lover' Kills Woman In Telangana, Claims She 'Refused To Return Money'
content: Locals caught hold of the accused before he could flee and thrashed him. Police said the suspect sustained serious injuries in the assault and was shifted to a hospital for treatment under police security.

title: PM Modi Sweden Visit LIVE: PM Modi To Visit Sweden Today On Third Leg Of Five-Nation Tour
content: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is on a five-nation tour from May 15 to 20, during which he visited the United Arab Emirates as part of the first leg of the visit.

title: Gautam Adani Inaugurates Eye Hospital In Bihar In A Boost To Rural Healthcare
content: The newly inaugurated hospital in Bihar's Saran district is designed to be a centre of excellence for ophthalmology in Bihar.

title: Georgia University Student Jumps Off Popular Cliff To Celebrate Graduation, Dies
content: Jayden Barreto was celebrating graduation with friends at the lake, a spot the website describes as a “must-do jump for adrenaline junkies and thrill seek

title: How NEET Paper Was Leaked By Insiders And Reached Aspirants Across States
content: The court submissions suggest that the NEET paper leak played out in phases, beginning with access inside the examination ecosystem.

title: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Tehran Prepares "Mechanism" For Hormuz Traffic, Israel Strikes Lebanon
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: FBI Jet, $35,000 Suite Date, Pearl Harbour Snorkel: Kash Patel Faces Heat
content: Several travels and related arrangements made on Kash Patel's orders included his girlfriend Alexis Wilkins, including a closed-door meeting on drug trafficking in Pennsylvania last month.

title: Man Who Struck Mumbai Teen, Left Her In Coma 9 Years Ago Fined Rs 20,000
content: P Narayanasamy Poosaripadaiyachi, now 66, was driving a Toyota Innova of the then railway commissioner when he hit Nidhi Jethmalani at a zebra crossing near Churchgate.

title: Nepal's Kami Rita Climbs Mount Everest For 32nd Time, Breaking His Own Record
content: The 56-year-old veteran climber reached the 8,849-metre peak at 10:12 am on Sunday, according to the Department of Tourism.

title: Video: Devotees Visit Bhojshala To Perform Puja After High Court Order
content: In its ruling, the High Court quashed a long-standing 2003 arrangement by the Archaeological Survey of India that had restricted Hindu worship to Tuesdays and permitted Friday prayers for the Muslim community.

title: Years Of Gang-Rape, Blackmail, Then 2 Sisters' Suicide In Jodhpur Horror
content: After the elder sibling died, the younger alleged that the accused started sexually assaulting her by blackmailing her with her sister's videos.

title: "Love You" Note Scribbled In Dust After Taps Stolen From Congress Office In Chhattisgarh
content: Visuals showed broken basins and flush tanks and faucet pipes strewn across restrooms in the Congress office in Ambikapur.
